channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Code scanning: Batch apply security alert suggestions on pull requests
item.description: GitHub code scanning alerts on pull requests are now easier to address with bulk actions. You can now apply fixes for code scanning alerts in the Files changed tab by… The post Code scanning: Batch apply security alert suggestions on pull requests appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Monthly news - April 2026
item.description: Microsoft DefenderMonthly news - April 2026 Edition This is our monthly "What's new" blog post, summarizing product updates and various new assets we released over the past month across our Defender products. In this edition, we are looking at all the goodness from March 2026. We are now including...
item.dc:creator: HeikeRitter

item.title: How do I completely uninstall applications on a mac?
item.description: Hi everyone! I'm a total newbie to the mac ecosystem, and I'm a bit confused about the proper way to remove software. Coming from Windows 10, I'm used to a dedicated uninstaller app, but on my Mac, I've mostly just been dragging app icons from the Applications folder into the Trash. I'm worried...
item.dc:creator: Stonehu

item.title: What is the best font changer for generating fancy fonts?
item.description: Look, I'm tired of plain text. I want those fancy Unicode fonts, the gothic, bubble, double-struck stuff but every font changer site I find is either broken or crawling with ads. What's the actual best font changer that gives you tons of styles and doesn't mess up when you copy-paste?Just need...
item.dc:creator: KellanWarner

item.title: iPhoneからAndroidにデータ移行はできますか？
item.description: 最近、新しいAndroidスマホに乗り換えようと思っているのですが、iPhoneからのデータ移行がうまくできるか不安です。写真や連絡先、メッセージなどをできるだけ簡単に移したいです。iphone android データ 移行のおすすめ方法があれば教えてください。特に、無料で安全にできる方法や、初心者でも失敗しにくいやり方を知りたいです。iphone android データ 移行を実際にやったことがある方、使ったツールや手順なども含めてアドバイスいただけると助かります。
item.dc:creator: Thatcherw

item.title: Discover and assess File Shares for migration to Azure Files with Azure Migrate
item.description: When organizations migrate to the cloud, applications are at the center of every decision. And behind every application is data, stored in file shares, accessed by users and services, and critical to keeping the application running. Moving that data seamlessly is not optional; it is a fundamental...
item.dc:creator: ankitsurkar

item.title: Why Data Platforms Must Become Intelligence Platforms for AI Agents to Work
item.description: The promise and the gap Your organization has invested in an AI agent. You ask it: "Prepare a summary of Q3 revenue by region, including year-over-year trends and top product lines." The agent finds revenue numbers in a SQL warehouse, product metadata in Dataverse, regional mappings in SharePoint,...
item.dc:creator: AnjaliSadhukhan

item.title: Webinar Cancellation
item.description: Hi everyone, The webinar “Re‑Envisioned: The New Single Device Experience in the Intune Admin Console,” originally scheduled for April 7 at 9:00 AM Pacific Time, has been cancelled at this time. We plan to reschedule the session, and when a new date is confirmed, it will be shared at...
item.dc:creator: emilyfalla

item.title: Agentic IIS Migration to Managed Instance on Azure App Service
item.description: Introduction Enterprises running ASP.NET Framework workloads on Windows Server with IIS face a familiar dilemma: modernize or stay put. The applications work, the infrastructure is stable, and nobody wants to be the person who breaks production during a cloud migration. But the cost of maintaining...
item.dc:creator: Gaurav-Seth

item.title: Copilot usage metrics now identify active and passive Copilot code review users
item.description: Copilot usage metrics now indicate which users have Copilot code review (CCR) activity, and whether that activity was active or passive. Enterprise and organization admins can see how users engage… The post Copilot usage metrics now identify active and passive Copilot code review users appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Introducing the New Microsoft Sentinel Logstash Output Plugin (Public Preview!)
item.description: Many organizations rely on Logstash as a flexible, trusted data pipeline for collecting, transforming, and forwarding logs from on-premises and hybrid environments. Microsoft Sentinel has long supported a Logstash output plugin, enabling customers to send data directly into Sentinel as part of...
item.dc:creator: JamesAde

item.title: Company Portal Profile installation failed on iPhone - Status code 400
item.description: Hello, I've been managing mobile devices through InTune for almost a year. Most of our devices are iOs - I add the phone to the Apple Business Manager - wait for it to appear in InTune - then install company portal, and log my user in. This pushed out software etc to the phone.I successfully set...
item.dc:creator: AmberH675

item.title: GitHub Copilot CLI combines model families for a second opinion
item.description: Discover how Rubber Duck provides a different perspective to GitHub Copilot CLI. The post GitHub Copilot CLI combines model families for a second opinion appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Nick McKenna

item.title: Announcing the end of support for Node.js 20.x in the Azure SDK for JavaScript
item.description: After July 9, 2026, the Azure SDK for JavaScript will no longer support Node.js 20.x. Upgrade to an Active Node.js Long Term Support (LTS) version to stay secure and up-to-date. The post Announcing the end of support for Node.js 20.x in the Azure SDK for JavaScript appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Minh-Anh Phan

item.title: Announcing Public Preview for Essential Machine Management
item.description: Managing servers and VMs across Azure, on premises, and multi-cloud environments often means turning on core capabilities—monitoring, updates, inventory, and configuration—through separate setup experiences. We’ve heard feedback that this makes it harder to get visibility into machine state and...
item.dc:creator: Meagan McCrory

item.title: Inside an AI‑enabled device code phishing campaign
item.description: A new wave of device code phishing shows how threat actors are scaling account compromise using AI and end‑to‑end automation. This campaign goes beyond traditional phishing by generating live authentication codes on demand, enabling higher success rates and sustained post‑compromise access. The...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Storm-1175 focuses gaze on vulnerable web-facing assets in high-tempo Medusa ransomware operations
item.description: The financially motivated cybercriminal threat actor Storm-1175 operates high-velocity ransomware campaigns that weaponize recently disclosed vulnerabilities to obtain initial access, exfiltrate data, and deploy Medusa ransomware. The post Storm-1175 focuses gaze on vulnerable web-facing assets in...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Threat Intelligence

item.title: Give your Foundry Agent Custom Tools with MCP Servers on Azure Functions
item.description: This blog post is for developers who have an MCP server deployed to Azure Functions and want to connect it to Microsoft Foundry agents. It walks through why you'd want to do this, the different authentication options available, and how to get your agent calling your MCP tools. Connect your MCP...
item.dc:creator: lily-ma

item.title: MCP Apps on Azure Functions: Quickstart with TypeScript
item.description: Learn how to build and deploy MCP (Model Context Protocol) apps on Azure Functions using TypeScript. This guide covers MCP tools, resources, local development, and serverless deployment with a practical weather app example. The post MCP Apps on Azure Functions: Quickstart with TypeScript appeared...
item.dc:creator: Swapnil Nagar

item.title: Logic Apps Aviators Newsletter - April 2026
item.description: In this issue: Ace Aviator of the Month News from our product group News from our community Ace Aviator of the Month April 2026's Ace Aviator: Marcelo Gomes Marcelo Gomes - Integration Team Leader @ COFCO International What’s your role and title? What are your responsibilities? I’m an Integration...
item.dc:creator: WSilveira

item.title: Introducing Migration assistant for SQL database in Fabric (Preview)
item.description: Modern organizations are at a crossroads. Legacy SQL Server workloads need modernization, but migration complexity often stands in the way. What if you could bring your SQL databases into a unified analytics platform—without the hassle of manual schema conversion, complex ETL pipelines, or costly...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Red Hat OpenShift: Managed Identity and Workload Identity now generally available
item.description: Azure Red Hat OpenShift now supports managed identities and workload identities as a generally available capability, so you can run OpenShift clusters and applications on Azure without long-lived service principal credentials.​ ARO, identity, and Azure governance With GA support for managed...
item.dc:creator: MelanieKraintz007

item.title: Use ANY_VALUE() for simpler grouping of results in Fabric Data Warehouse (Generally Available)
item.description: Fabric Data Warehouse now supports the ANY_VALUE() aggregate, making it easier to write readable, efficient T-SQL when you want to group by a key but still return descriptive columns that are functionally the same for every row in the group. What is ANY_VALUE()? ANY_VALUE() is an aggregate or...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Working with unstructured text in Fabric Data Warehouse with built-in AI functions (Preview)
item.description: Data warehouses traditionally focus on structured and semi-structured data. Free-form text such as notes, logs, or comments was difficult to process in data warehousing scenarios without external components. Microsoft Fabric Data Warehouse is opening new scenarios in modern data warehousing by...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Update on User Experience Feedback from the Reddit Thread (General Available)
item.description: Over the past few weeks, we’ve been actively reading through comments in the Reddit thread to gather UX feedback. Thank you all for taking the time to share your experiences — we appreciate your honesty and passion. Here’s a quick update on where things stand: Item dropdown no longer goes...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: How to Track Changes in Microsoft 365 Groups
item.description: An old PowerShell script tracked changes to Office 365 Groups. The techniques from 2016 wouldn’t be used today because features like the unified audit log didn’t exist then. We show what’s possible now by creating a new version of a Microsoft 365 Groups Change Report script to track additions,...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Microsoft ADO.NET Driver for Microsoft Fabric Data Engineering (Preview)
item.description: ADO.NET is a widely adopted data access technology in the .NET ecosystem that enables applications to connect to and work with data from databases and big data platforms. The Microsoft ADO.NET Driver for Fabric Data Engineering lets you connect, query, and manage Spark workloads in Microsoft Fabric...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Private DNS and Hub–Spoke Networking for Enterprise AI Workloads on Azure
item.description: Introduction As organizations deploy enterprise AI platforms on Azure, security requirements increasingly drive the adoption of private-first architectures. Private networking only Centralized firewalls or NVAs Hub–and–spoke virtual network architectures Private Endpoints for all PaaS services...
item.dc:creator: deepthihr

item.title: How we build Azure SRE Agent with agentic workflows
item.description: The Challenge: Ops is critical but takes time from innovation Microsoft operates always-on, mission-critical production systems at extraordinary scale. Thousands of services, millions of deployments, and constant change are the reality of modern cloud engineering. These are titan systems that power...
item.dc:creator: saziz_msft

item.title: Building MCP servers with Entra ID and pre-authorized clients
item.description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) gives AI agents a standard way to call external tools, but things get more complicated when those tools need to know who the user is. In this post, I’ll show how to build an MCP server with the Python FastMCP package that authenticates users with Microsoft Entra ID...
item.dc:creator: Pamela_Fox

item.title: Building Cost-Aware Azure Infrastructure Pipelines: Estimate Costs Before You Deploy
item.description: The Problem: Cost Is a Blind Spot in IaC Reviews Code reviews for Bicep or Terraform templates typically focus on correctness, security, and compliance. But cost is rarely part of the review process because: Developers don't have easy access to pricing data at review time Azure pricing depends on...
item.dc:creator: whosocurious

item.title: Windows 11 build 26200.8116 broke system dll crtdll.dll
item.description: Latest Windows 11 25H2 release broke this system dll by replacing it with msvcrt40.dll which isn't anywhere like it, and it broke 32 bit programs that relied on that dll to run since it is missing symbols.I couldn't find an appropriate space to report this kind of issues, since this is not user...
item.dc:creator: Edoardo

item.title: outlook.office.com
item.description: I canno´t login in to outlook.office.com. Get the error message:UTC Date: 2026-04-05T10:38:19.885ZClient Id: C0B69CD1666C4BDCAD56171753860EF7Session Id: e31a271e-c7b2-4f5f-abb5-854f463bfe2aClient Version: 20260320002.12BootResult: configurationBack Filled Errors: Unhandled Rejection: Error:...
item.dc:creator: rzs43

item.title: When the audio pipeline decides to act up 😂🤌
item.description: Ever since build 2820.x.x.x, I’ve been keeping an eye on one specific process — Audio Graph Isolation.And this little troublemaker 😂 sometimes decides it wants to “spice up your day.” Not by taking a few MB of RAM…Oh no, no — it goes straight for several gigabytes 😂 So you’re just sitting there,...
item.dc:creator: kikero_exe

item.title: MCP Apps on Azure Functions: Quick Start with TypeScript
item.description: Azure Functions makes hosting MCP apps simple: build locally, create a secure endpoint, and deploy fast with Azure Developer CLI (azd). This guide shows you how using a weather app example. What Are MCP Apps? MCP Apps let MCP servers return interactive HTML interfaces such as data visualizations,...
item.dc:creator: swapnil_nagar

item.title: Removing Old MS 365 Account
item.description: I had a Office 365 account under the discontinued educational organization license. The cloud storage was supposed to be disabled by MS at some point. So, I bought a personal Ofc 365 subscription. I tried to remove references to the old account and register my new license under a different email...
item.dc:creator: bcole1

item.title: Run the latest Azure Arc agent with Automatic Agent Upgrade (Public Preview)
item.description: Customers managing large fleets of Azure Arc servers need a scalable way to ensure the Azure Arc agent stays up to date without manual intervention. Per server configuration does not scale, and gaps in upgrade coverage can lead to operational drift, missed features, and delayed security updates. To...
item.dc:creator: yunishussein

item.title: Secure HTTP‑Only AKS Ingress with Azure Front Door Premium, Firewall DNAT, and Private AGIC
item.description: Reference architecture and runbook (Part 1: HTTP-only) for Hub-Spoke networking with private Application Gateway (AGIC), Azure Firewall DNAT, and Azure Front Door Premium (WAF) 0. When and Why to Use This Architecture Series note: This document is Part 1 and uses HTTP to keep the focus on routing...
item.dc:creator: kkaushal

item.title: Organization runner controls for Copilot cloud agent
item.description: Each time Copilot cloud agent works on a task, it starts a new development environment powered by GitHub Actions. By default, this runs on a standard GitHub-hosted runner, but teams… The post Organization runner controls for Copilot cloud agent appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: From Discovery to Executive Presentation: Plan Your Migration with Azure Migrate in Hours
item.description: The migration planning bottleneck Every migration starts with the same question: what do we have, and where should it go? Getting from that question to a clear, executive-ready answer has traditionally taken weeks. You scan the environment, export spreadsheets, manually classify workloads, run...
item.dc:creator: Shikher

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Sentinel: RSAC 2026
item.description: Security is entering a new era, one defined by explosive data growth, increasingly sophisticated threats, and the rise of AI-enabled operations. To keep pace, security teams need an AI-powered approach to collect, reason over, and act on security data at scale. At RSA Conference 2026 (RSAC), we’re...
item.dc:creator: spalani

item.title: GPT-5.1 Codex, GPT-5.1-Codex-Max, and GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini deprecated
item.description: We have deprecated the following models across all GitHub Copilot experiences (including Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions) on April 1, 2026. Model Deprecation date… The post GPT-5.1 Codex, GPT-5.1-Codex-Max, and GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini deprecated appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Announcing general availability of Network Security Perimeter for Azure Service Bus
item.description: Today we are excited to announce the general availability of Network Security Perimeter (NSP) support for Azure Service Bus. Network Security Perimeter allows you to define a logical network boundary around your Service Bus namespaces and other Azure PaaS resources, restricting public network...
item.dc:creator: EldertGrootenboer

item.title: The uphill climb of making diff lines performant
item.description: The path to better performance is often found in simplicity. The post The uphill climb of making diff lines performant appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Luke Ghenco

item.title: AKS cluster with AGIC hits the Azure Application Gateway backend pool limit (100)
item.description: I’m writing this article to document a real-world scaling issue we hit while exposing many applications from an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster using Application Gateway Ingress Controller (AGIC). The problem is easy to miss because Kubernetes resources keep applying successfully, but the...
item.dc:creator: kkaushal

item.title: Organization firewall settings for Copilot cloud agent
item.description: Copilot cloud agent includes a built-in agent firewall to control Copilot’s internet access and help protect against prompt injection and data exfiltration. Until now, the firewall was configured at the… The post Organization firewall settings for Copilot cloud agent appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Hello from New Hampshire 👋
item.description: Hi everyone, I’m Lissette. I work in enterprise HR technology and project delivery, with a strong focus on using Microsoft 365 and AI to help teams work more efficiently and scale what’s working.A lot of my day‑to‑day work sits at the intersection of technology, structure, and enablement,...
item.dc:creator: LissetteEspinalM365

item.title: Copilot cloud agent signs its commits
item.description: Copilot cloud agent now signs every commit it makes. Signed commits appear as Verified on GitHub, giving you confidence that the commit was genuinely made by the agent and hasn’t… The post Copilot cloud agent signs its commits appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Demystifying On-Demand Capacity Reservations
item.description: About On-Demand Capacity Reservations Introducing the “parking garage” metaphor There are dozens of VM types available in Azure which span multiple generations of CPU across vendors and architectures. Within each Azure region are datacenters hosting pools of hardware which runs Azure services, such...
item.dc:creator: KenHooverMSFT

item.title: Update KB5086672
item.description: I was told that the above-mentioned update could fix some recent startup issues I am having. Is there anywhere I could manually download this update or do I just need to wait until it is rolled out to my computer, as I am told it slowly being released?
item.dc:creator: BklynCane
